definitions.definition: The definite integral of a function f(x) with respect to x between the limits a and b is denoted by ∫[a, b] f(x) dx and is defined as the limit of the sum of the areas of the rectangles formed by dividing the area under the curve into n equal parts.
An improper integral is an integral that has an infinite discontinuity within the interval of integration or at one or both of the limits of integration.
The area between two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) over the interval [a, b] can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval.
The volume of a solid formed by revolving a region about the x-axis can be found by integrating the area of the disk/washer formed by revolving the region about the x-axis.
The volume of a solid formed by revolving a region about the y-axis can be found by integrating the area of the shell formed by revolving the region about the y-axis.
The surface area of a solid formed by revolving a region about the x-axis can be found by integrating the area of the surface formed by revolving the region about the x-axis.
The average value of a function f(x) over the interval [a, b] is given by the formula: (1/(b-a))∫[a, b] f(x) dx.
The median value of a function f(x) over the interval [a, b] is the value of the function at the point x = (a+b)/2.
The mean value of a function f(x) over the interval [a, b] is given by the formula: (1/(b-a))∫[a, b] f(x) dx.
The length of a curve y = f(x) over the interval [a, b] can be found by integrating the square root of the sum of the squares of the derivatives of the function.

definitions.term: Area of Region Bounded by a Curve and a Line
definitions.definition: The area of a region bounded by a curve y = f(x) and a line y = c over the interval [a, b] can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval.

definitions.term: Area between Two Curves in the First Quadrant
definitions.definition: The area between two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) in the first quadrant can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval [0, b].

definitions.term: Area between Two Curves in the Second Quadrant
definitions.definition: The area between two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) in the second quadrant can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval [0, -a].

definitions.term: Area between Two Curves in the Third Quadrant
definitions.definition: The area between two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) in the third quadrant can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval [-b, 0].

definitions.term: Area between Two Curves in the Fourth Quadrant
definitions.definition: The area between two curves y = f(x) and y = g(x) in the fourth quadrant can be found by integrating the difference between the two functions over the interval [-a, 0].

definitions.term: Volume of Solid of Revolution about the x-axis
definitions.definition: The volume of a solid formed by revolving a region about the x-axis can be found by integrating the area of the disk/washer formed by revolving the region about the x-axis.

definitions.term: Volume of Solid of Revolution about the y-axis
definitions.definition: The volume of a solid formed by revolving a region about the y-axis can be found by integrating the area of the shell formed by revolving the region about the y-axis.
